heading: Welcome
text: course introduces practical workflow accelerometer-based research: planning data collection, programming downloading devices, organizing files, processing raw data, checking quality, creating analysis-ready dataset.course uses ActiGraph examples, GGIR R, Stata reproducible workflow. principles broader one device software package.

heading: 0.1 How to use this course
text: module includes short lectures, optional practical activity, recommended resources, knowledge check. Follow modules order first pass; return software demonstrations apply workflow study.

text: need access research-grade accelerometer device-management software. Later modules use R/RStudio GGIR Stata (equivalent statistical package).Important: Device configuration, valid-day rules, wear-time criteria, thresholds, processing arguments must selected study population, device, wear location, protocol. course explains decision process; prescribe one universal parameter set.

heading: 1.2 Lesson 1.2: What can accelerometers measure?
text:
Accelerometer recordings can support estimates several dimensions movement behavior. exact outcome depends device, placement, algorithm, calibration, processing choices.Different monitor placements can useful different questions. example, thigh-worn monitor may better support posture classification, wrist placement often improves wear comfort continuous data collection. best placement follows research question protocol—universal rule.Video 1.2 — raw movement outcomes (target: 6–8 minutes)
Use diagram showing path signal → processing → quality control → outcomes. Include one concrete example, daily movement signal can contribute activity-volume estimate.

monitor initialized, study team must choose device model, wear location, start stop dates, instructions participants, criteria evaluating whether recording usable. decisions affect participant burden outcomes can validly interpreted.Protocol review required recording: Confirm wear schedule, sampling frequency, valid-day criteria, non-wear algorithm, reporting units, thresholds study’s principal investigator data lead. values prior cohort protocols examples, defaults every study.Video 1.3 — Choosing placement wear protocol (target: 7–9 minutes)
